Field Guidance: Keep AU labels explicit if the CSV needs to recreate the taxonomy YAML rather than just validate spec values.
This field is required to reconstruct org_structure.yaml accurately because each AU must map back to a segment.
Use when a spec needs to declare the governing assessment cycle explicitly.
Include the label row if the CSV needs to regenerate the taxonomy YAML, not just reference the ID.
Example:
assessment_period:
  id: fy25_annual
  label: FY25 Annual Risk Assessment

Field Guidance: Keep time windows centralized here so metric specs do not invent their own temporal logic.

Allowable Values (original): Cumulative count/value over the full FY25 Assessment Period|Point-in-time snapshot at the end of the Assessment Period
Field Guidance: Store the human-readable window labels explicitly if this sheet is expected to recreate taxonomy YAML.
Example:
time_windows:
  - id: assessment_period_cumulative
    label: Cumulative count/value over the full FY25 Assessment Period

Field Guidance: Use this as the canonical work-package routing field for a metric.

Allowable Values (original): Inherent Risk BDEs|Inherent Risk Supplemental Metrics|Control Assessment Metrics|Anti-Bribery Anti-Corruption Metrics
Field Guidance: Keep display labels explicit if the CSV is also acting as a source for taxonomy reconstruction.
Example:
work_packages:
  - id: wp_01
    label: Inherent Risk BDEs

Field Guidance: Pair this field with included or excluded AU lists depending on the selected strategy.

Allowable Values (original): Applies to all Assessable Units in org_structure.yaml|Applies to all AUs EXCEPT the specified 'excluded_aus' list|Applies ONLY to the explicitly listed AUs
Field Guidance: Keep the business-readable strategy descriptions explicit if round-tripping back to YAML matters.
Example:
applicability_strategies:
  - id: explicit_exclusion
    label: Applies to all AUs EXCEPT the specified 'excluded_aus' list

Field Guidance: Use this to standardize what customer or account population a metric is measured over.

Allowable Values (original): All customers/accounts within the Assessable Unit|Only customers rated via centralized Oracle CRR model|Only customers rated via decentralized manual processes
Field Guidance: Include these labels if the CSV needs to preserve business wording in the taxonomy YAML.
Example:
population_boundaries:
  - id: centralized_crr_only
    label: Only customers rated via centralized Oracle CRR model

Field Guidance: Store domains as a list because one metric can support more than one risk lens.

Allowable Values (original): AML Inherent Risk|Sanctions Inherent Risk|ABAC Inherent Risk
Field Guidance: Keep these labels explicit if the taxonomy sheet is a source for YAML generation.
Example:
risk_domains:
  - id: aml_ir
    label: AML Inherent Risk

Field Guidance: Use the methodology taxonomy ID, not a free-text category label.

Allowable Values (original): Customer Risk|Products & Services Risk|Transactional Risk|Delivery Channel Risk|Operational Geographic Risk|Emerging Risk
Field Guidance: Keep category labels explicit if this workbook must recreate the taxonomy YAML.
Example:
risk_categories:
  - id: customer_risk
    label: Customer Risk

Field Guidance: `factor_count` is part of the taxonomy YAML and should be preserved if the workbook is used as a reconstruction source.
Use this field to distinguish BDEs from supplemental and control metrics.
Keep display labels explicit if this workbook is expected to regenerate the taxonomy YAML.
Example:
metric_types:
  - id: base_data_element
    label: Base Data Element (BDE)

Allowable Values (original): Core quantitative metric for inherent risk calculations|Contextual data points supporting inherent risk profiles|Metrics assessing KYC, Investigations, or Reporting effectiveness
Field Guidance: Descriptions are not spec lookup values, but they are part of the taxonomy YAML and should be preserved for round-trip generation.
Example:
metric_types:
  - id: base_data_element
    description: Core quantitative metric for inherent risk calculations

Field Guidance: Use the source category to describe ownership of the upstream data, not the exact table name.
Keep category labels explicit if the workbook must recreate the taxonomy YAML.
Example:
data_source_categories:
  - id: rahona
    label: Rahona Data Platform

Field Guidance: Only use these system values when the selected source category is `rahona`.

Allowable Values (original): Business-managed side-loaded files and vendor extracts
Field Guidance: Descriptions are part of the taxonomy YAML and should be preserved if the workbook is the source for regeneration.
Example:
data_source_categories:

    description: Business-managed side-loaded files and vendor extracts
